headline_summary: Strait of Hormuz disruption persists with tanker incidents and large-scale anchoring reducing effective oil/LNG throughput.
COSCO Shipping suspends new bookings for Middle East routes, citing Hormuz traffic restrictions.
Israel orders evacuation across parts of southern Lebanon amid intensified hostilities with Hezbollah.
Energy prices and shipping/insurance costs are surging as U.S.-Iran hostilities expand and Gulf export logistics remain constrained.
Cyber activity has increased alongside kinetic escalation, with reported attacks on Iranian apps/websites.
risk_drivers: Sustained shipping disruption risk at the Strait of Hormuz (critical oil/LNG chokepoint)
Kinetic incidents impacting commercial vessels and Gulf infrastructure (projectiles/drones/missiles)
Broader regional escalation (Israel-Hezbollah hostilities in Lebanon)
Energy export/logistics constraints and elevated freight/insurance premia
Sanctions tightening/compliance risk on Iran-linked shipping and petroleum networks
Elevated cyber risk affecting critical infrastructure and financial sector

Energy: Higher upside risk to Brent/WTI and LNG due to Hormuz disruption and higher war-risk premia (oil_up_risk).
Elevated freight/insurance premia may keep delivered crude/products prices high, especially into Europe/Asia.
FX: Risk-off supports USD/CHF/JPY; EM FX pressure rises for energy importers (em_fx_pressure).
GCC pegs likely hold, but liquidity/forwards can reflect heightened risk if disruption persists.
Rates: Safe-haven demand can support core bonds, but energy-driven inflation risks may keep policy expectations tighter (rates_credit_spreads).
Credit spreads can widen for shipping, airlines, and energy-intensive sectors.
Equities: Risk-off pressure on global equities; relative tailwind for upstream energy/defense; headwind for transport/logistics (risk_off_flows).
Volatility elevated; shipping and infrastructure headlines can drive sharp sector rotations.

sources.title: Gulf shipping crisis deepens as tankers stranded for fifth day, US sinks Iranian warship
Iran conflict disrupts global shipping as tankers are stranded, damaged
COSCO Shipping suspends bookings for Middle East routes amid conflict
Israel orders evacuation of swathe of south Lebanon
Hackers hit Iranian apps, websites after US-Israeli strikes

events.title: Iranian missile strikes on commercial vessels
events.implication: Commercial-vessel strike risk raises odds of prolonged Hormuz disruption and higher war-risk premia.

events.title: Suspension of shipping operations through the Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Carrier suspensions and traffic restrictions tighten effective supply of freight capacity and slow Gulf energy exports.

events.title: COSCO Shipping suspends new bookings for Middle East routes
events.implication: Booking suspensions reflect escalating operational constraints that can compound trade delays and freight inflation.

events.title: Israel orders evacuation across parts of southern Lebanon amid Hezbollah hostilities
events.implication: A widening Lebanon front increases tail risk of broader regional escalation and additional disruption to Eastern Mediterranean energy/logistics.

events.title: Hackers hit Iranian apps and websites after U.S.-Israeli strikes
events.implication: Elevated cyber activity can increase operational risk for financial/telecom services and signal escalation beyond kinetic domains.

links.from: Iranian missile strikes on commercial vessels

links.why: Freight/insurance cost inflation and trade delays can pressure margins and financing conditions for transport/logistics and exposed corporates.

links.from: Suspension of shipping operations through the Strait of Hormuz
links.why: Higher delivered energy costs typically pressure EM importer currencies via worsened trade balances and inflation expectations.
